title: Tool permissioning models
description: Agents gate tools by allowlist, capability class, or per-call human approval, trading autonomy against blast radius.
body: Use when deciding how to constrain what an agent can do. An allowlist names exactly which tools are callable; a capability model groups tools by side-effect class (read, write, external) and grants per class; per-call approval routes side-effecting calls to a human. Stricter gating shrinks blast radius but reduces autonomy. Do not give an agent an open dispatch by function name, and avoid granting write/external tools without a gate. Actions: deny-by-default for unregistered tools and require an explicit grant for side effects. Validation signals include tests that an unregistered tool is denied and a side-effecting call without grant is blocked. Risk boundary: unrestricted tool access is a critical finding; require registry gating and human approval for high-impact tools.
